title: National policy on flu vaccination for older people
subtitle: This shows which countries have a national policy to recommend flu vaccines to older people.
caption: In 2020, national policies regarding flu vaccination for older people varied across the globe, with most countries recommending the vaccine while others did not. The map displays countries in two primary categories: those with a national policy recommending flu vaccines for older people, shown in blue, and those with no recommendation, shown in red. A significant portion of the world, including North America, most of South America, Europe, Australia, and parts of Asia, falls into the blue category, indicating a widespread recommendation. Conversely, several countries in Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Asia are marked in red, signifying the absence of a national recommendation. Notably, large areas of Africa and some regions in Asia are shaded in gray, indicating a lack of available data on their policies. The most prominent pattern is the broad adoption of flu vaccination recommendations for older people in many developed and developing nations, contrasted with the absence of such policies in specific regions and the data gaps in others.
